compiled_state.title: Test failures during commit workflow lack sub-agent resolution loop
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: When tests fail during the commit workflow, there is no automated resolution loop analogous to what exists for review failures. Review failures trigger a sub-agent resolution loop (up to review.max_resolution_attempts) that attempts to fix issues autonomously before escalating. Test failures should follow a similar pattern: detect the failure, dispatch a sub-agent to attempt a fix, re-run the failing test, and repeat up to a bounded number of attempts before escalating to the user.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Note: description uses feature-request language ('should follow a similar pattern', 'lack sub-agent resolution loop').
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Intent-contradicting: COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Steps 1 and 1.5 already document a test failure sub-agent resolution loop (detect failure → dispatch sub-agent → re-run → escalate). Introduced in commit 16573dc8 (2026-03-05). The reported absence of this loop is factually incorrect.
comments.author: Test
